title: Revisiting magnetoelectric response in collinear antiferromagnetic zigzag chains: A downfolding approach beyond conventional low-energy models
abstract: Magnetoelectric (ME) effects in antiferromagnets provide a fertile platform for exploring symmetry-driven cross-correlated responses. However, their microscopic origin remains elusive and is often obscured in simplified low-energy descriptions. In this study, we revisit the microscopic mechanism of the ME effect in a collinear antiferromagnetic zigzag chain by employing a multi-orbital tight-binding model that explicitly includes both $s$- and $p$-orbital degrees of freedom. Using analytical and numerical calculations based on the Kubo formula, we demonstrate that the ME response is governed by orbital degrees of freedom activated through $s$--$p$ hybridization, while the spin contribution vanishes due to spin conservation. To elucidate the low-energy description, we derive an effective Hamiltonian projected onto the $s$-orbital subspace using the Schur complement. We show that a naive application of the Kubo formula within this effective model fails to capture the ME response. This issue is resolved by systematically incorporating vertex corrections in terms of orbital hybridization into the response functions. Furthermore, by introducing a quasiparticle renormalization scheme, we formulate a renormalized Kubo formula that preserves conservation laws and accurately reproduces the full multi-orbital results. Our analysis revisits the conventional low-energy perspective and reveals that the ME effect originates from virtual interorbital processes encoded in vertex corrections, rather than from the bare low-energy Hamiltonian. The effective framework developed here provides a unified microscopic understanding of orbital-driven ME responses and offers a systematic route to incorporate hybridization effects beyond simple low-energy models.
summary: This paper provides a systematic framework for deriving accurate low-energy effective models for magnetoelectric responses in systems with orbital hybridization. It demonstrates that the ME effect in zigzag chains originates from virtual interorbital processes that must be captured via vertex corrections to satisfy fundamental conservation laws.
main_problem: Conventional low-energy effective models fail to capture the finite magnetoelectric (ME) response in collinear antiferromagnetic zigzag chains because they neglect high-energy orbital hybridization and necessary vertex corrections.
main_result: The authors develop a renormalized Kubo formula using a zeta-approximation that incorporates vertex corrections and hybridization, successfully reproducing the full multi-orbital ME response and preserving spin-z conservation.
method: The study employs a downfolding approach via the Schur complement to derive an effective Hamiltonian, combined with the Matsubara Green's function formalism and numerical analytic continuation.
model_or_system: A multi-orbital tight-binding model of a quasi-one-dimensional collinear antiferromagnetic zigzag chain, explicitly including both s- and p-orbital degrees of freedom and s-p hybridization.
key_observables: Magnetoelectric (ME) tensor component (alpha_z;y), orbital magnetization, and spin magnetization.
important_parameters: s-p energy separation (delta_p), apex angle (theta), hopping integrals (t_ss_sigma, t_sp_sigma), and AFM molecular field (phi).
main_assumptions: Neglects p-p orbital hopping; assumes the chemical potential lies outside the high-energy p-orbital window.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the model schematic; Figure 5 compares orbital and spin magnetization across different calculation methods; Figure 7 demonstrates the agreement between the effective and full models at both Matsubara and real frequencies.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the microscopic problem, defines the multi-orbital s-p model, derives the effective Hamiltonian via downfolding, identifies the failure of naive low-energy models, proposes a renormalized framework with vertex corrections (zeta-approximation), and validates the approach against the full model.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
